title: 📊 Engineering Overview [Edu] [v2]
description: High-level summary of all DORA metrics and Copilot adoption/impact indicators for the last 28 days.

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 📊 Engineering Overview

**All Four DORA Pillars — At a Glance**

## What it Shows
This overview dashboard presents the four core DORA (DevOps Research & Assessment) metrics side-by-side, giving engineering leaders a single-pane view of team health across throughput and stability.

| Metric | Pillar | What it Tracks |
|---|---|---|
| **Deployment Frequency** | Throughput | How often code ships to production |
| **Lead Time for Changes** | Throughput | Time from commit to production |
| **Change Failure Rate** | Stability | % of deployments causing incidents |
| **MTTR** | Stability | Time to restore service after failure |

## Why it Matters
DORA benchmarks are validated predictors of software delivery performance and organisational outcomes. High performers score well on *all four* metrics simultaneously — throughput gains must not come at the cost of stability.

## How to Use This Dashboard
- Use the stat panels to get an instant RAG (red/amber/green) status for each pillar
- Drill into any pillar's dedicated dashboard for trend charts and actionable detail
- Compare cohort breakdowns on the **DORA × Copilot** dashboard to assess Copilot's impact

panels.description: Engineering Overview — all four DORA pillars at a glance with drill-down links.

panels.title: Deployment Frequency

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id)::float / 4 AS "Deployments/Week"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.description: Source: GitHub Deployments API. GitHub-native only. 'Success' state is set by the deployer, not verified by GitHub.

panels.title: Lead Time (median hours)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600.0
) AS "Lead Time (hours)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.description: Source: GitHub Pulls + Deployments + bridge-resolver SHA matching. Unlinked PRs are excluded from median.

panels.title: Change Fail Rate

targets.rawSql:
WITH deploy_failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT df.deployment_id)::float /
  NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0) * 100 AS "Change Fail Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deploy_failures df ON df.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.description: Source: GitHub Issues (label='incident') + Deployments. Requires 'incident' label discipline — unlabeled incidents are invisible.

panels.title: MTTR (median)

targets.rawSql:
WITH failure_windows AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.created_at AS failed_at,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS recovered_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at)) / 3600.0
) AS "MTTR (hours)"
FROM failure_windows
WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL

panels.description: Source: GitHub Deployments API. PROXY: deploy gap, not actual service restoration time. Environment names must match exactly.

options.content:
## 📖 Deployment Frequency — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Counts distinct successful deployments divided by the number of weeks in the selected time window.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id)::float / 4 AS "Deployments/Week"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **GitHub-native only** — deployments via ArgoCD, Jenkins, or other tools not integrated with the GitHub Deployments API are invisible.
- **Environment field is free-text** — 'production', 'prod', and 'PROD' count as separate environments.
- **Success state is caller-set** — the deployer marks a deployment 'success'; GitHub does not independently verify it.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether deployed code is actually healthy in production.
- Deployments that bypassed the GitHub Deployments API.

## 📖 Lead Time (median hours) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` (merged) + `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + bridge resolver

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`pull_requests`, `deployments`, `deployment_pull_requests`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Median hours from PR merge to first successful deployment, linked via bridge resolver SHA-matching.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600.0
) AS "Lead Time (hours)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Bridge resolver dependency** — PRs linked by matching deployment SHA to PR merge_commit_sha (or head_sha for squash merges). SHA mismatch = PR excluded.
- **Multiple deployments per PR** — for multi-stage pipelines only the first link is used.
- **Median hides distribution** — use P90 alongside P50 for the full picture.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Stage breakdown: review vs CI vs deploy queue.
- Lead time for direct commits.

## 📖 Change Fail Rate — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/issues/issues)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues?labels=incident` + `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `issues`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Percentage of deployments that coincide with a GitHub Issue labeled 'incident' in the same time window.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH deploy_failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT df.deployment_id)::float /
  NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0) * 100 AS "Change Fail Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deploy_failures df ON df.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Label discipline required** — teams using PagerDuty, Jira, or Slack show 0% CFR even during real outages.
- **Correlation, not causation** — co-occurrence by time window, not an explicit deployment-incident link.
- **All environments weighted equally** — dev and production deployments count equally.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Severity or business impact of incidents.
- Incidents tracked outside GitHub Issues.

## 📖 MTTR (median) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Proxy: median time between a failed deployment and the next successful deployment in the same environment (SQL LEAD() window function).

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH failure_windows AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.created_at AS failed_at,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS recovered_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at)) / 3600.0
) AS "MTTR (hours)"
FROM failure_windows
WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Proxy, not actual restoration time** — measures deploy-gap. A config change or rollback that restores service without a new deployment is invisible.
- **LEAD() pairs each failure with the immediately next deployment** — stacked failures before a fix each measure to the same recovery.
- **Environment name is exact-match** — 'production' and 'prod' are separate partitions.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether users experienced an actual outage.
- Time spent on diagnosis, communication, or post-incident review.

panels.title: Active Copilot Seats

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.description: Source: GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats. Point-in-time snapshot. Active = last_activity_at within 28d (hardcoded). 403 → 0 silently.

panels.title: Acceptance Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1), 0) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics WHERE day >= NOW()::date - 28

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → download_links → NDJSON. Metric: loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum.

panels.title: Copilot PR Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.description: ⚠️ PROXY: PR author has active Copilot seat. GitHub provides NO per-PR Copilot usage signal. All ⚠️ panels use this proxy.

panels.title: Lines Accepted (28d)

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(loc_added_sum), 0) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)" FROM copilot_org_metrics WHERE day >= NOW()::date - 28

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → loc_added_sum. 28-day window ≠ Grafana time picker. Org-level aggregate only.

options.content:
## 📖 Active Copilot Seats — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Count of seats where last_activity_at >= NOW() - 28 days (hardcoded, NOT the Grafana time range).

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Point-in-time snapshot** — returns current state; no history.
- **28-day window is hardcoded** — always NOW()-28d, not the Grafana time picker.
- **403 = 0 silently** — insufficient permissions causes empty table with no error surfaced.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Frequency of Copilot use by each seat holder.
- Historical seat utilization over time.

## 📖 Acceptance Rate (%) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `loc_added_sum` / `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Sum of accepted lines divided by sum of suggested lines across all language breakdowns in the org 28-day report.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COALESCE(ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1), 0) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics WHERE day >= NOW()::date - 28
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Multi-line suggestion bias** — accepting a 10-line suggestion counts 10 even if developer only kept 2.
- **NDJSON response pattern** — endpoint returns a `download_links` envelope, not inline JSON. Must fetch the signed S3 URL and parse line-by-line.
- **Org-level aggregate only** — no per-repo or per-team breakdown.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether accepted code was later reverted.
- Acceptance rate by team, repository, or language.

## 📖 Copilot PR Rate (%) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal exists)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Percentage of merged PRs whose author has an active Copilot seat. ⚠️ Proxy — GitHub provides no per-PR Copilot usage signal.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Proxy, not direct measurement** — active seat is the only available signal; developer may have written this PR without Copilot.
- **Active seat ≠ used on this PR** — seat active within 28 days anywhere, not on this specific PR.
- **Seat revocation retroactivity** — revoked seat removes attribution on next sync.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- How much Copilot contributed to any specific PR.
- Code quality differences between attributed and non-attributed PRs.

## 📖 Lines Accepted (28d) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Sum of loc_added_sum across daily rows in the selected date range from the org 28-day report.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(loc_added_sum), 0) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)" FROM copilot_org_metrics WHERE day >= NOW()::date - 28
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **'Accepted' = kept at suggestion time** — developer can accept then immediately delete. Metric counts the accept event.
- **28-day rolling report** — covers last 28 days from today, not your Grafana time picker.
- **Org-level only** — no repo or team breakdown.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- How much accepted code is still in the codebase today.
- Which repositories or teams generated accepted lines.
